title: olearia coriacea
intro: Olearia coriacea, also known as the Jerisheim Daisy, is a flowering plant native to Australia, characterized by its white or pink flowers and hairy leaves. The plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it a potential treatment for various health issues. Studies suggest that Olearia coriacea has health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant activity. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Olearia coriacea are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
